title: gerardia tenuifolia subsp. polyphylla
intro: Gerardia tenuifolia subsp. polyphylla, also known as Gerard's Wood Betony or Wood Betony, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its slender stems and clusters of small, white, fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Gerardia tenuifolia subsp. polyphylla include its ability to soothe digestive issues, such as indigestion and nausea, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory symptoms like coughs and congestion. Gerardia tenuifolia subsp. polyphylla can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and has also been used in traditional herbal remedies to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.
